stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM from Meta.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The context involves standard NLP pipelines (text-generation) and common ML formats (transformers, safetensors).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The content highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags such as 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and utilization of Meta's latest LLM releases within the ML ecosystem.

stories.title: Actionable Activation Directions for Detecting and Mitigating Emergent Misalignment Across Language Model Families
stories.author: Abdul Rafay Syed
bullet_points: The research identifies that fine-tuning models on insecure code can cause emergent misalignment due to poorly understood internal structures.
The study proposes and validates an 'actionable activation-space direction' that correlates with this misalignment across multiple language model families.
This direction achieved a high separation rate (99.6%) between aligned and misaligned activations at the final layer of tested models.

stories.title: Moebius: 0.2B image inpainting model with 10B-level performance
stories.author: DSemba
bullet_points: Moebius is a new image inpainting model.
It is notable for achieving 10B-level performance.
The model is relatively efficient, operating with only 0.2B parameters.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational AI tasks.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like transformers and pytorch.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon.
The performance boost is attributed to updates in the MLX engine.
Improvements include faster response times, higher output quality, and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated support for MLX.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware for optimized performance.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating active development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging apps.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, allowing all processing to run on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing private, on-device AI assistance for coding tasks.

stories.title: How Anthropic may have talked itself into an AI export ban
stories.author: Clara Murray, Financial Times
bullet_points: Anthropic's public warnings about advanced AI risks are reportedly more pronounced than those from OpenAI.
The article speculates that Anthropic's cautionary stance may be influencing or leading to potential AI export restrictions.
This highlights a growing divergence in how major AI labs are framing the societal and regulatory risks of frontier models.

stories.title: FlowEdit: Associative Memory for Lifelong Pronunciation Adaptation in Flow-Matching TTS
stories.author: Nityanand Mathur
bullet_points: FlowEdit addresses the static nature of state-of-the-art flow-matching TTS systems, which fail on out-of-vocabulary words post-deployment.
It introduces a lifelong adaptation framework that allows pronunciation corrections without retraining the entire model.
The method achieves this by learning corrections as latent conditioning edits within the text embedding space, rather than updating model weights.

stories.title: StylisticBias: A Few Human Visual Cues Drive Most Social Biases in MLLMs
stories.author: Jana Diesner
bullet_points: Introduces 'StylisticBias,' a new benchmark to evaluate attribute-level social bias in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in current research by separating appearance-based bias from inherent identity differences in MLLMs.
Focuses on how subtle visual cues influence how MLLMs judge people, highlighting a significant societal risk in deployed AI.

stories.title: Your Mouse and Eyes Secretly Leak Your Preference: LLM Alignment using Implicit Feedback from Users
stories.author: Hamed Zamani
bullet_points: Proposes a new LLM alignment method that utilizes implicit user feedback instead of relying solely on expensive explicit human feedback.
Addresses the limitations of current alignment techniques, specifically the high cost and scarcity of explicit preference annotations.
Leverages implicit feedback, drawing a parallel to how large internet companies build their economic moats.

stories.title: Codex logging bug may write TBs to local SSDs
stories.author: vantareed
bullet_points: A potential logging bug exists within Codex (an AI model).
The bug has the critical vulnerability of potentially writing terabytes (TBs) of data to local SSDs.
This represents a significant security flaw in a major AI development tool.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware platforms.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically benefiting users on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The framework emphasizes a 'local-first' approach, allowing AI to run on user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating the use of local LLMs.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental image generation capabilities.
The feature is currently available for local execution on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Polymarket reportedly paid people to post fake videos of themselves placing bets
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Polymarket, a prediction market platform, is accused of paying users to create deceptive content.
The scheme involved individuals filming fake bets and celebrating fabricated wins on social media.
The Wall Street Journal investigation uncovered over 1,100 such deceptive clips.

stories.title: A US military exercise in space got underway with barely anyone noticing
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The US Space Force is focused on drastically accelerating satellite fielding timelines.
The goal is to reduce deployment time from years down to mere weeks, days, or hours.
The initiative is part of a broader effort to modernize and speed up military space capabilities.

stories.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers’ faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
stories.author: Matt Burgess, Maddy Varner, May Bulman, Gabriel Geiger, WIRED.com
bullet_points: The UK government plans to use facial scanning technology to verify the ages of asylum seekers.
The technology in question has been shown in tests to carry significant risks of making life-altering errors.
The decision to proceed with the flawed technology despite known risks is a major point of contention.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical need for LLM reasoning transparency to ensure model safety and debug behavior.
It investigates whether DiffusionGemma's computation in a continuous latent space impacts its reasoning transparency.
Transparency is decomposed into two components: variable transparency (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic transparency.

stories.title: How Do Instructions Shape Speech? Cross-Attention Attribution for Style-Captioned Text-to-Speech
stories.author: Sudarshan Kamath
bullet_points: The research addresses the lack of clarity on how individual words influence acoustic output in style-captioned Text-to-Speech (TTS) systems.
It introduces cross-attention attribution, adapting the DAAM framework to the speech domain for the first time.
The method generates per-token heatmaps across multiple layers of speech diffusion models to improve TTS controllability.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to manage structured task states for AI agents.
Addresses a key limitation in current agents where state (facts, constraints, conditions) must be reconstructed from the prompt each turn.
Focuses on improving policy-adherence in complex domains like customer service by explicitly tracking state across tool calls.

stories.title: DeepSWIP: Quotient-WMC Counterfactuals for Neural Probabilistic Logic Programs
stories.author: Fengxiang He
bullet_points: DeepSWIP addresses a limitation in neurosymbolic systems (like DeepProbLog) by introducing counterfactual reasoning capabilities.
The system provides a 'single-world counterfactual semantics' to handle causal semantics for interventions and evidence.
It achieves this by using neural materialization to map fixed-context neural predicates to standard ProbLog choices and applying SWIPs.

stories.title: SARLO-80: Worldwide Slant SAR Language Optic Dataset 80cm
stories.author: Georgia Channing
bullet_points: SARLO-80 introduces a new, large-scale dataset designed to bridge the gap between optical and Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) data.
Current SAR datasets are limited, often using low-resolution, intensity-only products that lack complex-valued measurements and native acquisition geometry.
The dataset aims to enable more physically grounded multimodal learning for foundation models in remote sensing applications.

stories.title: Sovereign Execution Brokers: Enforcing Certificate-Bound Authority in Agentic Control Planes
stories.author: Deying Yu
bullet_points: Addresses the security gap where autonomous agents' non-deterministic reasoning processes control production mutation.
Introduces the Sovereign Execution Broker (SEB) as a runtime enforcement boundary for certified authority.
Moves beyond traditional identity-based access control by providing a mandatory enforcement point for certified actions.

stories.title: Multi-LCB: Extending LiveCodeBench to Multiple Programming Languages
stories.author: Dmitrii Babaev
bullet_points: The paper introduces Multi-LCB, an extension of the LiveCodeBench (LCB) benchmark.
LCB is a recognized tool for evaluating LLMs on code generation, currently limited to Python.
Multi-LCB aims to address this limitation by extending the benchmark to support multiple programming languages, testing LLM generalization.

stories.title: How Transparent is DiffusionGemma?
stories.author: Neel Nanda
bullet_points: The paper investigates the transparency of DiffusionGemma, an LLM utilizing a continuous latent space for computation.
Model reasoning transparency is highlighted as crucial for debugging, mitigating misuse, and ensuring alignment in LLMs.
Transparency is decomposed into 'variable transparency' (understanding intermediate states) and algorithmic aspects.

stories.title: Predictability as a Fine-Grained Measure for Privacy
stories.author: Karthik Sridharan
bullet_points: Introduces 'privacy via predictability' as a fine-grained alternative to traditional Differential Privacy (DP).
Addresses the costly worst-case privacy-accuracy tradeoff inherent in standard DP mechanisms.
Explicitly incorporates attacker knowledge, compromised data portions, and query families into the privacy measure.

stories.title: Execution-State Capsules: Graph-Bound Execution-State Checkpoint and Restore for Low-Latency, Small-Batch, On-Device Physical-AI Serving
stories.author: Liang Su
bullet_points: Addresses a gap in current LLM serving by focusing on low-latency, small-batch, on-device scenarios, contrasting with high-throughput systems.
Proposes 'Execution-State Capsules' to manage the full execution state, not just the traditional KV cache, which is crucial for interactive agents.
Targets use cases like interactive LLM agents, speech systems, and robot policies that require frequent branching and tight responsiveness budgets.

stories.title: Probe-and-Refine Tuning of Repository Guidance for Coding Agents
stories.author: Jeannie Albrecht
bullet_points: The paper addresses the need for LLM-based coding agents to possess high-level operational knowledge about a software repository beyond just the code itself.
It investigates the effectiveness of using supplementary context files (like AGENTS.md) to guide these coding agents, noting existing conflicting research.
The proposed method involves 'Probe-and-Refine Tuning' to improve how agents utilize repository guidance for better coding performance.

stories.title: LedgerAgent: Structured State for Policy-Adherent Tool-Calling Agents
stories.author: Chitta Baral
bullet_points: Introduces LedgerAgent, a framework designed to give policy-adherent agents a structured state for tool-calling.
Addresses a limitation in standard agents where task state (facts, constraints, conditions) is implicitly reconstructed from the prompt across turns.
Focuses on improving reliability in complex domains like customer service by explicitly managing state alongside tool interactions and policy adherence.

stories.title: Scalable Training of Spatially Grounded 2D Vision-Language Models for Radiology
stories.author: Thomas Brox
bullet_points: Introduces RefRad2D, a large-scale, bilingual (German/English) dataset of 1.2M CT/MR image-text pairs for radiology.
Develops RefRad2D using automated curation and LLM-based methods to avoid manual spatial annotations.
Presents RadGrounder, a model capable of joint report generation, VQA, and spatial grounding in medical imaging.

stories.title: CATCH-ME if you RAG: a dataset of Contextually Annotated multi-Turn Counterspeech against Hate and Misinformation Exchanges
stories.author: Marco Guerini
bullet_points: Addresses the overlap between hate speech and misinformation, a common challenge in online discourse.
Proposes a new dataset, 'CATCH-ME if you RAG,' to provide high-quality, contextually annotated examples for counterspeech generation.
Aims to improve LLM performance in generating non-repetitive and effective counterspeech against complex, multi-turn exchanges.

stories.title: Token-Operations-Oriented Inference Optimization Techniques for Large Models
stories.author: Qinghuai Ma
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical need for optimizing large model inference for scalable and low-cost deployment.
It introduces a novel four-layer technical architecture for token-oriented inference optimization.
The architecture comprises Multi-model Fusion, Model Optimization, Compute-Model Fusion, and Compute-Network-Model Fusion.

stories.title: PsyScore: A Psychometrically-Aware Framework for Trait-Adaptive Essay Scoring and ZPD-Scaffolded Feedback
stories.author: Chanjin Zheng
bullet_points: Introduces PsyScore, a framework designed to integrate psychometrics into Automated Essay Scoring (AES).
Addresses the limitation of current systems where scoring and feedback are treated separately, often lacking learner-specific context.
Aims to provide both reliable assessment and actionable, proficiency-level-aware instructional feedback using LLMs.

stories.title: The Register Gap: A Meaning Intelligence Framework for Nigerian Public Discourse
stories.author: Celestine Achi
bullet_points: Introduces the Meaning Intelligence Framework (MIF) to analyze Nigerian public discourse.
MIF is a nine-dimension schema designed to distinguish true communicative intent from surface sentiment.
Highlights that AI failure in Nigerian languages is primarily due to context failure, not just translation issues.

stories.title: When Software Started Writing Software: A Developer’s History of AI
stories.author: adamthedeveloper
bullet_points: The article discusses the historical shift in software development driven by AI.
It addresses the noticeable impact of AI on modern job descriptions for developers.
The content offers a personal narrative perspective on the integration of AI into the industry.

stories.title: The AI Security Gap: Why your autonomous agents are completely unprotected
stories.author: magopredator
bullet_points: Focuses on the critical security vulnerabilities inherent in autonomous AI agents.
Highlights the current lack of robust protection mechanisms for these agents.
Addresses a growing, niche, but highly relevant area of AI safety and security.
